mappings.standardControlTitle: Source is version controlled and reviewed
mappings.notes: Feature branches, pull requests, validation evidence, review gates, and main-branch merge gates support controlled source changes.
mappings.gapSummary: Branch protection enforcement remains a GitHub setting rather than repository code, but the control evidence supports the intended source-review flow.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Builds run from trusted automation with short-lived credentials
mappings.notes: Image CI runs in GitHub Actions, assumes AWS roles with OIDC, and publishes immutable commit-SHA images to authorized ECR repositories.
mappings.gapSummary: No explicit gap recorded for the current image build trust boundary.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Provenance exists and can be verified
mappings.notes: Images are tagged by commit SHA and GitOps manifests are updated from the build workflow, but no signed provenance or artifact attestation is produced yet.
mappings.gapSummary: Generate and verify signed build provenance/attestations for published images.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Build dependencies are pinned or controlled
mappings.notes: Major third-party GitHub Actions are pinned to immutable revisions and static validation runs before mutable deployment workflows.
mappings.gapSummary: No explicit gap recorded for pinned workflow actions.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Build and release credentials are isolated
mappings.notes: AWS credentials are short-lived and deploy/destroy workflows use an environment gate. Manifest promotion now uses a scoped GitHub App token and PR gate instead of direct workflow-token writes to main, but the GitHub App private key remains a sensitive repository secret.
mappings.gapSummary: Keep the GitHub App installation narrowly scoped and consider external token brokering or additional approval for promotion writes.

mappings.notes: ECR scan-on-push and Trivy HIGH/CRITICAL reports exist, but scans are advisory-only and exception approval is not enforced.
mappings.gapSummary: Fail or require approved exceptions for high-risk findings before images become trusted deployment inputs.
